mission.title: Install and Configure Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
mission.description: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) is an enterprise Kubernetes distribution that provides a robust platform for developing and deploying containerized applications. It is ideal for organizations looking to leverage Kubernetes with additional features such as developer tools, enhanced security, and integrated CI/CD pipelines.

steps.title: Install OpenShift CLI
steps.description:
Download and install the OpenShift CLI (oc) for version 4.17. Use the following commands to download and install: 
```bash
curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/4.17.0/openshift-client-linux-4.17.0.tar.gz
sudo tar -xvf openshift-client-linux-4.17.0.tar.gz -C /usr/local/bin/ 
rm -f openshift-client-linux-4.17.0.tar.gz
```

steps.title: Create an OpenShift Cluster
steps.description:
Use the OpenShift Installer to create a new cluster. Run the following command: 
```bash
openshift-install create cluster --dir=<installation-directory> --log-level=info
``` 
Ensure you have the necessary cloud credentials set up in the installation directory.

steps.title: Configure kubectl
steps.description:
After the cluster is created, configure kubectl to connect to your OpenShift cluster: 
```bash
export KUBECONFIG=<installation-directory>/auth/kubeconfig
kubectl cluster-info
``` 
This command should return the cluster information.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Installation
steps.description:
Verify that the cluster is up and running by checking the node status: 
```bash
kubectl get nodes
``` 
All nodes should be in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up monitoring and autoscaling by enabling the Cluster Autoscaler: 
```bash
oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-autoscaler/master/deploy/cluster-autoscaler-operator.yaml
``` 
This will enable the autoscaler for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Remove OpenShift Cluster
uninstall.description:
To uninstall the OpenShift cluster, run the following command: 
```bash
openshift-install destroy cluster --dir=<installation-directory> --log-level=info
``` 
This will remove all resources associated with the cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade OpenShift Cluster
upgrade.description:
To upgrade from version 4.16 to 4.17, first ensure you have a backup of your cluster: 
```bash
oc adm must-gather --image=quay.io/openshift-release-dev/ocp-v4.0-art-dev:latest
``` 
Then, update the cluster version by editing the ClusterVersion resource: 
```bash
oc edit clusterversion version
``` 
Change the spec to the desired version: 
```yaml
spec:
  desired:
    version: 4.17.0
``` 
Monitor the upgrade process with: 
```bash
oc get clusterversion
```

troubleshooting.title: Cluster Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the cluster fails to start, check the installation logs: 
```bash
cat <installation-directory>/install.log
``` 
Look for errors related to cloud provider configuration or resource limits.

troubleshooting.title: Node Not Ready
troubleshooting.description:
If a node is not in 'Ready' state, check the node conditions: 
```bash
kubectl describe node <node-name>
``` 
Look for any conditions that indicate issues with the node.

troubleshooting.title: oc CLI Commands Failing
troubleshooting.description:
If oc commands are failing, ensure that your KUBECONFIG is set correctly: 
```bash
echo $KUBECONFIG
``` 
If it is not set, export it again using: 
```bash
export KUBECONFIG=<installation-directory>/auth/kubeconfig
```

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for Kubernetes 1.30, enhanced security features, and improved developer experience.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of certain legacy APIs.

versionNotes.changes: Added new monitoring capabilities and improved performance for large clusters.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of OpenShift should show all nodes in 'Ready' state and allow you to run kubectl commands without errors. The cluster should also be configured for autoscaling and monitoring.
codeSnippets:
curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/4.17.0/openshift-client-linux-4.17.0.tar.gz
sudo tar -xvf openshift-client-linux-4.17.0.tar.gz -C /usr/local/bin/ 
rm -f openshift-client-linux-4.17.0.tar.gz

prerequisites.cloudCLI: gcloud, aws, az, or oci depending on your cloud provider
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the OpenShift CLI and kubectl installed, along with the necessary cloud provider CLI tools.
